issue.title: Evaluate Python Android framework candidates with explicit selection criteria

comment.body:
@Ma7moud3ly thanks for the detailed inquiry! 

>However, I'm not fully certain if in this milestone scope intended to use abcvlib from a Python script🤔

It doesn't necessarily have to expose everything from abcvlib, but if, for example, it can implement the same things the `basicSubscriber` or `basicAssembler` apps do, that would be enough. Essentially there needs to be a way in python to read the inputs and control the outputs of the system:

Inputs = Data from any of the publisher classes
Outputs = setWheelOutput, generate QR Code

Your example code above would be too low-level for the needs. Python shouldn't  need to touch anything related to the serial connection code itself. All of that init code should remain on the Android side and run prior to any python code starting. You might think of it like sandboxing the python code to the abcvlibMainLoop. 

Let me know if that makes any sense and if I can expand on anything to make the goal any clearer.
